briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the MST/SST example using the XDpRxSs driver.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort RX Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if DisplayPort RX Subsystem configured in SST/MST successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if DisplayPort RX Subsystem failed to configure in SST/MST.

briefdescription.para: This function initialize required platform specific peripherals.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if required peripherals are initialized and configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function configures Video Phy.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if Video Phy configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function configures DisplayPort RX Subsystem.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if DPRX Subsystem configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up the interrupt system so interrupts can occur for the DisplayPort RX Subsystem core.

detaileddescription.para: The function is application-specific since the actual system may or may not have an interrupt controller. The DPRX Subsystem core could be directly connected to a processor without an interrupt controller. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if interrupt setup was successful.
A specific error code defined in "xstatus.h" if an error occurs.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a video mode change interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a no video interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a vertical blank interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a training lost interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when the training done interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a down request interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a down reply interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when the unplug event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when the link bandwidth change occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for PLL reset request.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up a custom timer which the driver will use for MicroBlaze systems.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if a timer controller exists for use.

simplesect.para: A timer controller must be present in the system.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to override the driver's default sleep functionality.

detaileddescription.para: For MicroBlaze systems, the XDpRxSs_WaitUs driver function's default behavior is to use the MB_Sleep function from microblaze_sleep.h, which is implemented in software and only has millisecond accuracy. For this reason, using a hardware timer is preferable. For ARM/Zynq SoC systems, the SoC's timer is used - XDpRxSs_WaitUs will ignore this custom timer handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs core instance.
is the number of microseconds to delay/sleep for.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpRxSs_SetUserTimerHandler driver function to set this function as the handler for when the XDpRxSs_WaitUs driver function is called.

briefdescription.para: This function will present the resolution of the incoming video stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpRxSs instance.

simplesect.para: The resolution will be rounded up to the nearest resolution present in the XVidC_VideoTimingModes table.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for XDpRxSs SST/MST example.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the MST/SST example passed.
XST_FAILURE if the MST/SST example was unsuccessful.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the XDpRxSs driver in single stream (SST) transport or multi-stream transport (MST) mode.

simplesect.para: For this example to display output, the user need to implement initialization of the system (DpRxSs_PlatformInit), Video Phy (DpRxSs_VideoPhyInit), start DisplayPort RX subsystem (XDpRxSs_Start) and DisplayPort RX Subsystem setup (DpRxSs_Setup). The input to the Subsystem is from RX (GT). This example requires an interrupt controller in the system. The functions DpRxSs_PlatformInit, DpRxSs_VideoPhyInit and DpRxSs_Setup are declared and are left up to the user to implement.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 07/29/15 Initial release. 2.00 sha 10/05/15 Changed DpTxSs_Setup > DpRxSs_Setup. 4.00 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
